ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general ramen noodle dish class; no specific Yokohama-style entry exists. FOODON_00002700 used as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a Yokohama-specific ramen code. A0CKP (noodle soup dish) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Yokohama Special Ramen or ramen noodle soup as a food concept at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Yokohama Special Ramen is approximately 400 g, comprising noodles, pork slices, broth, and toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Yokohama Special (pork soup ramen) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Yokohama Special (cr_id G00010593), a canonical Singapore HPB record with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values preserved from the source record. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork-based ramen soup (NDB No. 22899) as the closest approved analogue, yielding approximately 1.3 g saturated fat per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Noodle soup (wheat noodles in broth) — analogous estimate based on wheat noodle GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yokohama Special Ramen. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for wheat-based noodle soups and boiled wheat noodles, which range from 47–58. The very low carbohydrate content of this dish (1.58 g/100 g; 6.3 g per 400 g serving) means the glycemic load per serving is negligible at approximately 3.5. GL calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_per_serving_g / 100 = 55 × 6.32 / 100 = 3.5. A GI of 55 is considered low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1149 mg per 100 g — nearly double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; a standard 400 g bowl delivers approximately 4596 mg sodium, more than double the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request low-sodium soy sauce, reduced broth volume, and avoid adding any table seasoning, and monitor total daily sodium intake carefully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate content is very low at 1.58 g/100 g with sugar at 1.54 g/100 g; high protein at 19.04 g/100 g supports glycaemic stability and satiety.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given its negligible carbohydrate and high protein content; however, patients should confirm preparation does not include added sugars via mirin or sweet marinades, and avoid pairing with white rice to prevent glycaemic load escalation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 1149 mg/100 g poses a significant cardiovascular risk through blood pressure elevation; cholesterol at 54 mg/100 g and estimated saturated fat at 1.3 g/100 g contribute additional atherogenic load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as a high-sodium, moderate-cholesterol food and limit portion size to no more than 150–200 g, request low-sodium broth, and avoid consuming the full bowl of broth to reduce sodium and cholesterol intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 1149 mg/100 g far exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold, directly worsening RA-related tissue swelling; pork protein contributes arachidonic acid which amplifies inflammatory pathways.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in standard preparation; if consumed, request low-sodium soy sauce and consider substituting pork with chicken or fish to reduce arachidonic acid load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yokohama Special Ramen is a Japanese-style pork soup ramen dish served in Singapore, catalogued in the Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database under food group 'Grains and staples' with subgroup 'Noodle/Pasta-based dishes'. The dish consists of wheat-based ramen noodles served in a rich pork bone broth (tonkotsu-style or soy-seasoned pork broth), typically garnished with sliced chashu pork, bamboo shoots (menma), nori seaweed, spring onions, and sesame oil. The broth is deeply savoury and umami-forward, with a silky, slightly fatty mouthfeel. The noodles are springy and chewy. Nutritionally, this dish is notable for its very high protein content (19.04 g/100 g) and extremely high sodium content (1149 mg/100 g), driven by soy sauce and seasoned pork broth. Carbohydrates are very low at 1.58 g/100 g, making the dish glycaemically benign in isolation. Fat is moderate at 3.87 g/100 g. Cholesterol is 54 mg/100 g. The dish is clinically unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium load, but is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given its low carbohydrate and high protein profile. A standard serving of approximately 400 g delivers around 4596 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily sodium limit of 2000 mg. The dish is not halal-certified due to pork content. It is a popular lunch and dinner option in Japanese ramen restaurants in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in Yokohama ramen?
Can diabetics eat Yokohama Special Ramen?
Is Yokohama ramen suitable for hypertension?
What are the calories in Yokohama Special Ramen?

Yokohama Special pork soup ramen nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo canonical database entry for Yokohama Special (cr_id G00010593) with HPB-assigned confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy pork ramen analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle analogues and documented. All mandatory fields are complete.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this dish; this is a confirmed HPB-reported value and is not a zero-macro justification issue as dietary fibre is not a core macro. Core macros (protein_g 19.04, fat_g 3.87, carbohydrate_g 1.58) are all non-zero and sourced from HPB. saturated_fat_g of 1.3 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork-based ramen soup analogue (NDB No. 22899) as HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Yokohama Special preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. The dish is not halal due to pork content. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Yokohama Special Ramen exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycaemic load per serving is negligible (3.5) due to very low carbohydrate content. Multilingual local names for Chinese (横滨特别拉面 / 橫濱特別拉麵), Malay (Mi Ramen Khas Yokohama), Tamil (யோகோஹாமா ஸ்பெஷல் ராமன்), and Hindi (योकोहामा स्पेशल रामेन) added to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements for this dish as served in Singapore context; these are transliterations and descriptive translations of the dish name and should be verified by a native-language reviewer.
